channel.title: The Grant Desk - NL
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: NL.

item.title: Stichting DOEN: Funding for Pioneering Culture and Social Initiatives (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting DOEN

Location: Netherlands-based fund (backed by the Dutch charity lotteries); themes have differing geographic focus areas. Legal entities only.

Award: No fixed amounts published; funding is tailored per initiative. No application fee.

Duration: Apply at least four months before the project start date; the application procedure takes about four months.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Stichting DOEN, funded by the Dutch charity lotteries, supports pioneering initiatives within themes including the transformative power of imagination (art and culture), regenerative economy and solidarity economy. Applications are accepted year-round via the Mijn DOEN portal from legal entities only (no individuals) and must be received at least four months before the project starts; the assessment procedure takes about four months. Amateur art practice and standalone productions of films, books, CDs or websites are not supported. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fonds voor Cultuurparticipatie: Open Schemes for Cultural Institutions 2025-2028 (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Fonds voor Cultuurparticipatie

Location: Netherlands and the Caribbean parts of the Kingdom. Cultural institutions, vocational education institutions and interdisciplinary makers.

Award: EUR 5,000 to EUR 75,000 depending on the scheme (e.g. cultural education in mbo EUR 5,000-55,000, in vmbo/vso/pro EUR 10,000-65,000, design-based learning platforms up to EUR 75,000, interdisciplinary makers EUR 12,500-50,000). No application fee.

Duration: Schemes run within the 2025-2028 policy period; multiple schemes currently open.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Fonds voor Cultuurparticipatie, one of the Dutch national cultural funds (annual budget around EUR 30 million), currently has several schemes open for applications: cultural education in vocational education (mbo, EUR 5,000-55,000), cultural education in vmbo/vso/pro (EUR 10,000-65,000), cultural education for the Caribbean parts of the Kingdom (EUR 5,000-50,000), an open call for design-based learning platforms (up to EUR 75,000) and an interdisciplinary scheme for professional makers (EUR 12,500-50,000). Most schemes target cultural institutions, in some cases in partnership with schools. See the subsidies overview for per-scheme conditions. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: VSBfonds: Kunst & Cultuur Donations (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: VSBfonds

Location: European Netherlands. Private legal entities (foundations, associations); applicants without a registered legal form are also eligible.

Award: No fixed minimum or maximum; the requested amount must be in proportion to other funding sources. No application fee.

Duration: Project funding; apply at least four months before the start date (two months for amateur arts initiatives).

Deadline: Rolling / undated

VSBfonds supports cultural initiatives that make it possible for everyone in the Netherlands to take part in art and culture, spanning ten programme lines from professional performances to community arts. Applications are accepted year-round with no deadlines from foundations, associations and applicants without a formal legal form, and must be submitted at least four months before the initiative starts (two months for amateur arts). A two-minute online quickscan checks eligibility before the formal application. No fixed minimum or maximum amount; the request should be proportionate to other funding. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIDN fonds: Pioneer Projects for a Strong Internet (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: SIDN fonds

Location: Netherlands. Open to natural persons and legal entities for the pioneer track; theme calls require legal entities.

Award: Up to EUR 10,000 per pioneer project; theme calls (once or twice a year) fund larger projects above EUR 10,000. No application fee.

Duration: Pioneer projects develop an idea into a demo, pilot or experimental design; continuous intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

SIDN fonds (the foundation behind the .nl domain registry) invests in daring projects with societal added value that contribute to a strong internet and strong internet users. The Pioneers track is continuously open and awards up to EUR 10,000 to develop an innovative idea into a demo, pilot or experimental design; it is open to both individuals and legal entities. Larger projects are funded through theme calls issued once or twice a year (legal entities only). Projects must show innovation and public value and share their results openly; plain website or app building and purely B2B proposals are excluded. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Delfina Foundation Residency 2027 (London)
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund / Delfina Foundation

Location: London, UK. For visual artists and curators based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom, funded by the Mondriaan Fund.

Award: Subsidy of EUR 9,188 for an artist or EUR 7,232 for a curator/art observer toward travel, material and living costs (a 10 percent personal contribution is automatically deducted by the Mondriaan Fund); studio, living space and Delfina Foundation support are paid directly by the Mondriaan Fund. Contribution for Caribbean-based artists can be increased for higher travel costs. No application fee.

Duration: Three months: 27 September to 17 December 2027

Deadline: 27 Aug 2026

Mondriaan Fund-supported working period at London's Delfina Foundation, one of the UK's best known artist-in-residence programmes, housed at 29-31 Catherine Place near Buckingham Palace with space for 6 to 8 international residents (own room, shared kitchen and facilities). Delfina promotes artistic exchange and experiment, offering visual artists and curators a platform to carry out research, develop ideas and position their practice within the global discourse, with introductions to the London art scene and Delfina's international network. The programme includes visits to museums, galleries and non-profit platforms, discussions with London-based artists, portfolio reviews with curators and regular networking lunches; residents are expected to take part. Applicants are not asked for a concrete project proposal but must submit a clear motivation: why they wish to take part, what research they want to conduct, and how it can contribute to Delfina's ongoing programme. WHO: visual artists and curators based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom; duo applications not possible; application must be in English. One work period available: 27 September to 17 December 2027. Selection by an advisory committee with a Delfina representative; processing may exceed three months. DEADLINE: Thursday 27 August 2026, before 16:00 Dutch time / 11:00 Caribbean time.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fonds 21: Kunst & Cultuur Project Grants (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Fonds 21

Location: Netherlands. Dutch foundations, associations and non-profit BVs/NVs; projects must reach a broad and/or new audience in the Netherlands.

Award: Minimum request EUR 10,000, no stated maximum; around EUR 11.5 million awarded per year across all pillars. No application fee.

Duration: Project funding; apply at least four months before the project starts.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Fonds 21 supports professional, high-quality art and culture projects by Dutch non-profit organizations (foundations, associations and non-profit BVs/NVs), across performing arts, visual arts, film, literature and interdisciplinary work, with additional pillars for arts education and youth & society. The minimum request is EUR 10,000 with no stated maximum, and roughly EUR 11.5 million is available annually. Applications are accepted year-round via the online portal (after a short eligibility snelcheck) and must be submitted at least four months before the project start date. Free-of-charge events are generally not supported; the fund expects most programming to be paid-access. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Het Cultuurfonds: Project Grants for Foundations and Associations (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Het Cultuurfonds (formerly Prins Bernhard Cultuurfonds)

Location: Netherlands. National office plus provincial chapters; projects in culture, nature and science.

Award: Typically EUR 15,000 to EUR 50,000 for national project applications by foundations and associations; smaller amounts via provincial chapters and themed funds (EUR 1,500 to EUR 25,000). Individual makers and collectives can request up to EUR 5,000. No application fee.

Duration: Project funding; decision within roughly 2 weeks to 3 months depending on the route.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Het Cultuurfonds (formerly Prins Bernhard Cultuurfonds) invests over EUR 40 million per year in culture and nature in the Netherlands. Foundations and associations can apply year-round for project grants (typically EUR 15,000 to EUR 50,000 at national level), and since 2025 individual makers and collectives can apply for up to EUR 5,000. Provincial chapters run their own quarterly rounds for regional projects, and numerous themed funds (EUR 5,000 to EUR 25,000) cover specific disciplines. Rolling intake via the online application portal. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Rijkscultuurfondsen: Interdisciplinary Grant
item.description:
Organisation: Dutch national cultural funds (Rijkscultuurfondsen), via the Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands. For professional makers and organisations based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom whose practice spans multiple disciplines.

Award: EUR 12,500 to 50,000 per project. The individual-maker track is approximately EUR 24,900 for 12 months (income cap EUR 55,000 including the grant).

Duration: Up to 12 months for the individual-maker track.

Deadline: 01 Oct 2026

The Interdisciplinary grant is a new joint scheme of the six Dutch national cultural funds (Mondriaan Fund, Performing Arts Fund NL, Fund for Cultural Participation, Netherlands Film Fund, Creative Industries Fund NL, and the Dutch Foundation for Literature), announced on 7 July 2026. It supports makers and organisations whose work genuinely crosses the boundaries of the individual funds' disciplines (visual art, design, film, literature, performing arts, and cultural participation) and cannot be contained within a single discipline. TWO TRACKS: an Individual track for professional makers with a demonstrable interdisciplinary practice (at least one year of experience; income cap EUR 55,000 including the grant; approximately EUR 24,900 for 12 months); and an Organisation track for organisations running multi-discipline projects. AMOUNT: EUR 12,500 to 50,000 per project. ELIGIBILITY: makers and organisations based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. APPLYING: handled through the Mondriaan Fund's online application portal (info@mondriaanfonds.nl); an account is required in advance. DEADLINE: 1 October 2026, 16:00 Dutch time (single application round).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund (Mondriaan Fonds)

Location: Netherlands. Applicants must be based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom; residencies may take place in the Netherlands or abroad.

Award: Grant amount varies depending on the location and duration of the residency. Applicants must contribute at least 10% themselves (personal investment, third-party contributions, an interest-free loan, or crowdfunding).

Duration: Varies by residency.

Deadline: 27 Aug 2026

The Mondriaan Fund's Residency grant supports professional visual artists, curators, and art observers who want to deepen their practice through a residency in the Netherlands or abroad. It contributes to the costs of a self-arranged residency that offers meaningful development for the applicant's work. WHO CAN APPLY: visual artists, curators, and art observers who have been professionally active for at least one year and are based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. AMOUNT: the grant amount varies depending on the residency's location and duration; applicants must contribute at least 10% of the costs themselves, through personal investment, third-party contributions, an interest-free loan, or crowdfunding. APPLYING: submitted through the Mondriaan Fund's online application portal, which requires a pre-created account (allow several working days). DEADLINE: 27 August 2026, 16:00 Dutch time (11:00 Caribbean time).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Publication
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund (Mondriaan Fonds)

Location: Netherlands. Featured artist(s) must be professional visual artists based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom; publishers may co-apply.

Award: Contribution of up to 35% of eligible expenses. Total budget for this round is EUR 205,000.

Duration: Project-based (production of a publication).

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

The Mondriaan Fund's Publication grant supports the production of special publications about living visual artists from the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. It funds books and other publications that give substantial, high-quality attention to an artist's work and practice. WHO CAN APPLY: visual artists and publishers, individually or jointly; the featured artist(s) must be professional and have been active for at least one year, based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. AMOUNT: the Mondriaan Fund contributes a maximum of 35% of the eligible expenses; the available budget for this round is EUR 205,000. APPLYING: submitted through the Mondriaan Fund's online application portal, which requires a pre-created account (allow several working days to set up). DEADLINE: 30 July 2026, 16:00 Dutch time (10:00 Caribbean time).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AIxDESIGN x KB: Open Call for Sessions, (Art)works and Performances
item.description:
Organisation: AIxDESIGN, co-presented with the Koninklijke Bibliotheek (KB), National Library of the Netherlands

Location: KB, The Hague, Netherlands (in-person exhibition and programme); pitches welcome from people based anywhere

Award: Fees by track: opening-night experience EUR 1,500 to 2,000; a session in the events programme EUR 2,000 to 2,500; an existing (art)work for the exhibition EUR 200 to 400 honorarium (including licensing and production). The fee stays the same wherever you are based.

Duration: Exhibition runs November 2026 to February 2027; opening event November 2026

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

AIxDESIGN and the KB (Koninklijke Bibliotheek, the National Library of the Netherlands) are building an exhibition and public programme about AI, made from the national collection, at the KB in The Hague. Nadia Piet is Creative Lead with the AIxDESIGN community, co-presented with the KB. The premise: long before neural nets, libraries were the machines we built to organise human knowledge, and the show uses the KB's collection to look at what AI is, where it comes from, and how it connects to the older story of how we store, sort, and search what we know. Part of the show lives in the KB Atelier, with talks, workshops, and screenings alongside, intended as AI literacy you can wander into. WHAT THEY WANT: work that engages AI in some way (critical, creative, hands-on, or playful); bonus if it also speaks to archives, libraries, or how we store and sort knowledge, though you do not need to touch the KB's collection to take part. You may pitch to more than one track: (A) SOMETHING FOR THE OPENING NIGHT, a performance, talk, screening, tour, ritual, booth, game, or other experience for the November 2026 opening, fee EUR 1,500 to 2,000; (B) A SESSION IN THE EVENTS PROGRAMME, a workshop or other session once during the three-month run, around 2.5 to 4 hours, fee EUR 2,000 to 2,500; (C) AN (ART)WORK FOR THE EXHIBITION, existing work in any format (installation, sculpture, zine stand, a corner of your own) sturdy enough for a public area with daily traffic, honorarium EUR 200 to 400 including licensing and production; (D) SOMETHING ELSE ENTIRELY, pitch it anyway. WHO: artists, designers, researchers, technologists, performers, musicians, archivists, or educators working around AI, data, libraries, or cultural heritage; no finished idea or polished CV needed, just a clear, specific pitch. You can be based anywhere and they especially welcome people outside the Netherlands; the show is in-person in The Hague and the fee stays the same wherever you are coming from. HOW TO APPLY: send a short pitch through the form (which track, your idea in a paragraph or two, roughly what it would need, and a link to your work); takes about five minutes, no full application required. Deadline: end of August 2026. Questions to hello@aixdesign.co.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NEU Commission 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Page Not Found

Location: The Netherlands; open to non-EEA-national students enrolled at Dutch art academies.

Award: EUR 2,000 each to three commissioned writers, paid as an advance in September 2026.

Duration: One-time commission; commissioned texts published at a later date

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Page Not Found announces the third edition of the NEU Commission, which intends to support the emergence of alternative voices within contemporary art. It targets a group currently under-represented in Europe: art students with limited financial means who are required to provide a guarantee for their visa and to pay high non-EU tuition fees, economical barriers to their participation in art and knowledge production in The Netherlands. Page Not Found will commission three art students from non-European countries to each write an essay or a free-form text in English. Each selected writer receives EUR 2,000 for the commission, provided as an advance payment in September 2026; the commissioned texts are published at a later date. OPEN CALL: only students enrolled in Dutch art academies who are NOT nationals of the European Economic Area are eligible. The commissioned text does not need to be submitted when answering the open call; the jury selects recipients based on their previous writing and financial need. Review the Rules of the Open Call and the FAQ before applying. Deadline: Friday 31 July 2026, 23:59 CEST (Amsterdam).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: KB Researcher-in-Residence (RiR) 2027
item.description:
Organisation: KB, National Library of the Netherlands

Location: The Hague, Netherlands (two days per week on-site at the KB premises)

Award: Secondment at 0.5 FTE for 6 months based on your current salary, up to EUR 5,590 gross per month for a full-time-equivalent position, plus travel expenses, office facilities, and support from a research software engineer, research advisor, and collection and data specialists. No application fee.

Duration: 6 months at 0.5 FTE (two days per week on-site); two positions in 2027, starting Q1 and Q3

Deadline: 27 Sep 2026

ELIGIBILITY: two Researcher-in-Residence positions in 2027. Open to early-career researchers of any discipline (data science, social science, computer science, humanities) who are currently working on a PhD or within 5 years of their PhD end date. You must have, or be able to secure, an affiliation with a Dutch university or research institute willing to second you; external PhD students may use the funding to obtain such a contract. Your project must make use of one or more of the KB digital collections and you must be able to work with primarily Dutch data. You must be available at 0.5 FTE over 6 months and able to spend two days a week on-site at the KB in The Hague. COLLECTIONS: over 130 million digitized pages of newspapers, magazines, radio bulletins and books from 1470 onward (via Delpher and DBNL), a born-digital web collection of more than 23,000 websites, ground-truth and derived datasets on the KB Lab, and metadata including Linked Data. APPLY: use the official proposal template and submit a single PDF by 27 September 2026 to dh@kb.nl, after reading the call's terms and conditions; questions to dh@kb.nl. TIMELINE: deadline 27 September 2026; internal eligibility review followed by an external committee review; outcome notified November 2026; residencies start Q1 and Q3 2027. DISCIPLINES: digital humanities / data science / computational research with cultural-heritage collections. NOTE: the output is usually a dataset or tool prototype hosted on the KB Lab; you agree to share results via blog posts on the KB Lab and to publish resulting tools or data under an open license within 6 months of the residency. Proposals should align with the KB Research Agenda or the Cultural AI Lab.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stichting Stokroos: Seed Grant 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting Stokroos

Location: Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname; must have lived and worked there at least one year. Applicants outside the Randstad, or plans taking place there, particularly encouraged.

Award: Fixed grant (no project budget required): EUR 5,000 for an individual, EUR 7,500 for a duo, EUR 10,000 for a collective. Paid in full within 2 weeks of award. No application fee.

Duration: Focused working period; plan starts at least 10 weeks after submission and runs a maximum of one year.

Deadline: 31 Oct 2026

The Stichting Stokroos Seed Grant gives individual makers, duos and collectives time and space to focus on their artistic development, for example a focused working period for research and experiment, or the development of a prototype, new work or methodology. There are two application rounds in 2026: 15 May to 30 June 2026 (now full) and 1 September to 31 October 2026; Stokroos considers a maximum of 60 eligible applications per period (or closes once the budget is reached) and supports around 50 applications per year. Eligibility: active as a visual artist, designer and/or craft practitioner (overlap possible); living and working in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year; at least 21 years old; you completed your first subject-specific (vocational or higher) programme between 2018 and 2022, or started without formal training (first presentation marks the start of your practice); not currently enrolled in a study or post-academic programme. Duos and collectives may apply provided all participants meet the guidelines. The grant is a fixed amount with no budget required: EUR 5,000 (individual), EUR 7,500 (duo) or EUR 10,000 (collective). Plans can take place anywhere, start at least 10 weeks after submission and last a maximum of one year. You may apply for the Seed Grant once every two calendar years and receive it up to twice, and may submit only one application per year across all Stokroos grants. Apply via the online form (Dutch or English) with a portfolio PDF up to 20 pages/person, optional video up to 10 minutes total, CV up to 2 pages/person, and work/plan/collaboration descriptions up to 2,500 characters each. Stokroos aims to respond within 8 weeks; no appeal is possible. Next deadline: 31 October 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stichting Stokroos: Residency at Fundaziun Nairs 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting Stokroos

Location: Residency in Scuol, Switzerland (Fundaziun Nairs). Applicants must live and work in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year. Applicants outside the Randstad particularly encouraged.

Award: Fully supported stay (accommodation and meals paid directly to Fundaziun Nairs) plus an unrestricted living, travel and production budget of EUR 7,000. 80% paid before the residency, the remainder on receipt of a brief final report. No application fee.

Duration: Intensive three-month working period (July through September).

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

Stichting Stokroos funds a three-month reflective residency at Fundaziun Nairs in Scuol, Switzerland, for one selected visual artist, designer or craft practitioner (overlap possible). Both emerging and established makers may apply. Stokroos covers full accommodation and meals directly with Fundaziun Nairs and provides a discretionary EUR 7,000 budget for living, travel and production. The application should include a proposal that engages specifically with the residency, its Alpine surroundings and/or its historical and cultural context. Eligibility: you completed your first subject-specific (vocational or higher) programme in 2022 or earlier, or have an equivalent established practice, or started without formal training (first presentation marks the start of your practice); at least 21 years old; living and working in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year; not currently enrolled in a study or post-academic programme. Applications are individual only; duos and collectives cannot apply. Makers already selected by Fundaziun Nairs may still apply if they meet the criteria. You may submit only one application per year across all Stokroos grants, and must not have received a Seed Grant rejection in the previous calendar year. Apply via the online form (Dutch or English) with a portfolio PDF up to 20 pages, optional video up to 10 minutes total, CV up to 2 pages, and work/plan descriptions up to 2,500 characters each. Selection includes an introductory interview with up to three candidates; Stokroos aims to respond within 8 weeks. No appeal is possible. Application window: 1 to 11 September 2026. More information at nairs.ch.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: WBSO: R&D Tax Credit (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Rijksdienst voor Ondernemend Nederland (RVO)

Location: Netherlands. Dutch companies and self-employed paying payroll or income tax that conduct qualifying R&D.

Award: Payroll/income tax reduction for R&D, not a cash grant. 2026: 36 percent of the first EUR 380,000 of R&D wage costs (50 percent for startups), 16 percent above; fixed deduction for self-employed. National budget EUR 1.817 billion for 2026. No application fee.

Duration: Applied for in advance per period; cannot start before approval.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The WBSO (Wet Bevordering Speur- en Ontwikkelingswerk) is the Dutch R&D tax credit administered by RVO. It reduces payroll/wage tax for companies with R&D staff and provides an income-tax deduction for self-employed people doing R&D; it is a tax credit, not a cash grant. For 2026 the benefit is 36 percent of the first EUR 380,000 of R&D wage and other costs (50 percent for qualifying startups) and 16 percent above that; self-employed receive a fixed deduction (and need at least 500 R&D hours per year). Eligible applicants are Dutch companies and self-employed people paying payroll or income tax who carry out qualifying R&D in the EU. Applications are made in advance via Mijn RVO and cannot be retroactive; for coverage of the final three months of a year the deadline is 30 September. Time-keeping and project administration are required. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Cultuur+Ondernemen: Cultuurlening (rolling loan, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Cultuur+Ondernemen

Location: Netherlands. Creative and cultural professionals and organisations residing and working in NL.

Award: Loan (must be repaid), up to EUR 500,000. Interest 2 percent up to EUR 50,000, then 2-5 percent (risk-based) above EUR 50,000; term up to 10 years. This is a LOAN, not a grant. No application fee.

Duration: Loan term up to 10 years; rolling.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Cultuurlening from Cultuur+Ondernemen is a low-interest loan (not a grant) for creative and cultural professionals and organisations in the Netherlands, to invest in their practice or business. Loans run up to EUR 500,000, with interest of 2 percent up to EUR 50,000 and 2-5 percent (risk-based) above EUR 50,000, and a term of up to 10 years. The loan must be repaid. Eligible applicants reside and work in the Netherlands and are either art-school graduates or have at least three years of professional practice (others can apply with a motivation and reference). Applications are accepted on a rolling basis with no fixed deadline. No application fee. Note: separate regional products exist (for example the Rotterdam and Amsterdam Cultuurlening) with different rates and caps.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stichting Stokroos: Maker Initiatives Grant 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting Stokroos

Location: Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname; must have lived and worked there at least one year. Applicants outside the Randstad, or plans taking place there, are particularly encouraged.

Award: Fixed grant of EUR 25,000 or EUR 40,000 (chosen depending on need). Maximum participant fee EUR 2,200/month (40-hour week); third-party hire capped at EUR 85/hour excl. VAT. Co-funding not required. No application fee.

Duration: Plan starts in the next calendar year (2027) and runs a maximum of two years.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Stichting Stokroos Maker Initiatives supports established visual artists, designers and craft practitioners (overlap possible), including duos and collectives, in developing and realising a multivocal artistic project or programme centred on a common concern, in collaboration with other makers or communities. There is one application round per year and Stokroos can support 4 to 6 proposals; a maximum of 40 eligible applications are considered per period. Eligibility: you completed your first subject-specific (vocational or higher) programme in 2020 or earlier, or have an equivalent established track record, or started without formal training (first presentation marks the start of your practice); at least 21 years old; living and working in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year; not currently enrolled in a study or post-academic programme. You must apply together with an artistic or creative partner (from professional makers to communities); duos and collectives must also have an external partner. You may submit only one application per year across all Stokroos grants, and must not have received a Seed Grant rejection in the previous calendar year. The grant is a fixed contribution of EUR 25,000 or EUR 40,000 depending on need. Plans can take place anywhere, start in the next calendar year and last a maximum of two years. Applications are submitted via the online form in Dutch or English (portfolio PDF up to 20 pages/person, optional video up to 10 minutes total, CV up to 2 pages/person, budget up to 3 pages, work/plan/collaboration descriptions up to 2,500 characters each). Stokroos aims to respond within 10 weeks; no appeal is possible. Application window: 20 to 31 July 2026. Questions: mail@stokroos.nl.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: UNIIQ: Proof-of-Concept Fund (rolling, Zuid-Holland)
item.description:
Organisation: UNIIQ

Location: Zuid-Holland (South Holland), Netherlands only. Early-stage research-driven startups.

Award: Around EUR 300,000 per company as a convertible loan (investment, not a grant). Total fund EUR 50 million. No application fee.

Duration: Investment via convertible loan; rolling intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

UNIIQ is a proof-of-concept investment fund for the earliest, riskiest phase of company building in Zuid-Holland (South Holland). It invests around EUR 300,000 per company as a convertible loan (for example redeemed over about three years or converted at a follow-on valuation), drawn from a EUR 50 million fund. It is an investment, not a grant. Eligible companies are early-stage, research-driven startups based in Zuid-Holland, typically in life sciences, deep tech and high tech. Intake is continuous via an orientation meeting followed by a funding application. No application fee.

Open call details

item.title: ROM InWest: Startup and Scale-up Financing (rolling, Noord-Holland)

Organisation: ROM InWest (Regional Development Company for Noord-Holland)

Location: Noord-Holland (North Holland), Netherlands. Startups, scale-ups and innovative SMEs.

Award: Financing of EUR 300,000 to EUR 5,000,000 as loans, equity participations or a combination (not a gift); cannot exceed 49.9 percent of a financing round. This is investment, not a grant. No application fee.

Duration: Investment; rolling intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

ROM InWest is the regional development company for Noord-Holland (North Holland). It finances startups, scale-ups and innovative SMEs working on food, energy, healthcare and circular-economy transitions, providing EUR 300,000 to EUR 5,000,000 as loans, equity participations or a combination (explicitly not a gift), and not exceeding 49.9 percent of a financing round. It is an investment, not a grant. Companies must be based in Noord-Holland. Intake is continuous, starting with a quick-scan tool. No application fee.

item.title: Amarte Fonds: Development and Project Contributions (rolling)
item.description:
Organisation: Amarte Fonds

Location: Netherlands and the Caribbean part of the Kingdom (the ABC and SSS islands). Applicants must be based there.

Award: Development contribution up to EUR 2,750; project contribution up to EUR 5,500. Up to 80 percent paid in advance, remainder after the report is approved. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; rolling decisions.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Amarte Fonds is a private Dutch foundation that supports the arts, with rolling (continuous) contributions for visual artists and independent curators. There are two contribution types: a development contribution of up to EUR 2,750 and a project contribution of up to EUR 5,500. Up to 80 percent is paid in advance, with the remainder after the final report is approved. Applicants must be based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom (the ABC and SSS islands). The fund also supports work across theatre, film, music, literature and interdisciplinary projects through specific programmes. There is no fixed deadline and no application fee; decisions are relatively fast. Apply year-round via the online portal.

Open call details

item.title: MIT R&D Collaboration Projects 2026 (MKB Innovatiestimulering Topsectoren)
item.description:
Organisation: Rijksdienst voor Ondernemend Nederland (RVO)

Location: Netherlands. SME consortia (minimum two collaborating SMEs).

Award: Grant covering 35 percent of eligible costs. Small projects EUR 50,000-200,000; large projects EUR 200,000-350,000 per project (EUR 25,000-100,000 or EUR 25,000-175,000 per participant). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; ranked tender (not first-come).

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

The MIT R&D Collaboration Projects scheme (MIT R&D-samenwerkingsprojecten, part of MKB Innovatiestimulering Topsectoren) funds joint R&D projects by Dutch SMEs within the national Top Sectors and Mission-driven Innovation policy. The grant covers 35 percent of eligible costs: small projects of EUR 50,000-200,000 and large projects of EUR 200,000-350,000 (with per-participant ranges of EUR 25,000-100,000 for small and EUR 25,000-175,000 for large). Eligible applicants are consortia of at least two collaborating SMEs, with no single participant carrying more than 70 percent of costs. The national RVO round runs 9 June to 15 September 2026 (closes 17:00) and is a ranked tender, not first-come-first-served; some provinces run separate regional MIT windows. No application fee. Apply via RVO.

item.title: Expo Bart: OPEN HALL 2026 (Free Project Space, Technique & Support)
item.description:
Organisation: Expo Bart

Location: Expo Bart, NYMA, Waalplein 6, Nijmegen, Netherlands

Award: No fee and no rent, but also no artist fee or other compensation (geen vergoeding). Provides free use of a 100 m2 fully blackout-able project space, limited technique (2 short-throw beamers, a PA set with mixer and microphones, and light spots) and helping hands, plus promotion of your event via Expo Bart's channels.

Duration: Programme runs 13 November to 20 December 2026; book one or more days (open Thursday to Sunday, 12:00-17:00; other times negotiable)

Deadline: Closed 12 Jul 2026

Do you have an idea you have always wanted to carry out but can never find the space for? Expo Bart in Nijmegen invites you to apply for OPEN HALL. From 13 November to 20 December 2026, Expo Bart makes its project space, technique and helping hands available to help realise your idea, whether that is a week-long exhibition, a one-day workshop, a film shoot, a knitting club, a concert series, a film night, a performance, a dinner, a combination of all of these, or something completely different. PRACTICAL: you can sign up for one or more days between 13 November and 20 December 2026; Expo Bart is open Thursday to Sunday from 12:00 to 17:00 (organising something outside opening hours can be discussed together); the 100 m2 project space is fully blackout-able; limited technique is available, namely two short-throw beamers, a PA set (with mixer and microphones) and light spots; and your event is of course shared via Expo Bart's channels. There is no compensation and no rent is charged. APPLY by sending your idea (max 1 A4, including photos) to info@expobart.nl with the subject line 'OPEN HALL', stating your plan/idea, your preferred day(s), and what you need in terms of production. Deadline: 12 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Digital Culture Grant Scheme 2026 (final round)
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands. Requires Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK) registration.

Award: EUR 10,000-25,000 for solo projects; EUR 10,000-50,000 for collaborative projects with partners. At least 20 percent of total project costs must be covered by co-financing. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based.

Deadline: 19 Aug 2026

The Stimuleringsfonds Digital Culture Grant Scheme (Deelregeling Digitale cultuur) supports projects by makers, researchers, design studios and cultural institutions positioned professionally in digital culture. Solo projects can receive EUR 10,000-25,000; collaborative projects with partners can receive up to EUR 50,000. At least 20 percent of total project costs must be covered by co-financing. Applicants must be registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK). The 2026 application window for this round runs 12-19 August 2026 (opens 12 August 15:00, closes 19 August 16:00 CEST); in 2026 the scheme runs only two rounds and this is the last. No application fee. Apply via the official scheme page.

Open call details

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds + Film Fund: Immerse\Interact 2026 (second round)
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie and the Netherlands Film Fund

Location: Netherlands. Requires Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK) registration.

Award: Development: EUR 15,000 for individual makers (plus up to EUR 5,000 coaching, max EUR 20,000) or EUR 25,000 for producers (plus up to EUR 5,000 coaching). Realisation: up to EUR 35,000 for individual makers or up to EUR 125,000 for producers (Fund covers max 85 percent). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based, development and realisation phases.

Deadline: 25 Aug 2026

Immerse\Interact is a joint grant scheme of the Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie and the Netherlands Film Fund for immersive and interactive media projects (VR, MR, AR and other interactive storytelling). It supports artistic projects with a supporting cinematic component, in which fiction or non-fiction storytelling in relation to interactive (web-based/mobile) or immersive technologies (XR, room-scale projections) is central. It funds both development and realisation. Development support is EUR 15,000 for individual makers (plus up to EUR 5,000 coaching, capped at EUR 20,000 total) or EUR 25,000 for producers (plus up to EUR 5,000 coaching); the coaching contribution requires a motivation from both applicant and coach plus the coach's CV, and the development phase must result in a usable prototype. A maximum of six development contributions are awarded per round, to keep the development and realisation budgets in balance. Realisation support is up to EUR 35,000 for individual makers or up to EUR 125,000 for producers (the Fund covers a maximum of 85 percent of the production budget); note that at realisation stage the producer may not also be the director, screenwriter or designer. A separate Presentation Procedure offers presentation grants for projects previously supported by Immerse\Interact after 1 January 2022. EUR 600,000 is available per Fund in 2026. Open to professional makers, collectives and producers with a demonstrable track record in immersive or interactive media; applicants must be registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK). The second 2026 round runs 24 June to 25 August 2026 (opens 24 June 15:00, closes 25 August 16:00 CEST). No application fee. Questions: immerseinteract@stimuleringsfonds.nl.

Open call details

item.title: Sandberg Instituut: Call for Proposals, Temporary Department 2027-2029 (Head of Department, The Archive)
item.description:
Organisation: Sandberg Instituut

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands; applicants must be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate for the duration of the programme.

Award: Freelance position as Head of the Temporary Department, approx. two days per week over two academic years (September 2027 to July 2029), plus allocated hours for curriculum development (October 2026 to July 2027). Approximate daily rate EUR 553.60. The department is also supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a dedicated department coordinator plus administrative support, and institutional infrastructure and guidance. A modest fee is provided for the second-round interview stage. No application fee.

Duration: Two-year Temporary Department, September 2027 to July 2029 (curriculum development from October 2026)

Deadline: Closed 05 Jul 2026

Sandberg Instituut invites artists, designers, researchers, makers and educators to propose a Temporary Department, taking on the role of Head of Department for the period September 2027 to July 2029. THEME, THE ARCHIVE: for this edition the institute seeks proposals that engage with the archive as a contemporary artistic, social, political and pedagogical framework. Increasingly recognised as a site of power, selection, memory, erasure and imagination, the archive is approached here not as a static collection but as a living, contested and generative process to be activated, reinterpreted and transformed through contemporary practice, and as a material and methodological field to work with, against and through. Possible starting points include: archives as sites of resistance, repair and restitution; embodied, oral and performative archives; collective and community-based archives; digital, algorithmic and AI-driven processes; counter-archives and practices of refusal; speculative archives and future-oriented forms of memory; creative practices that activate, reinterpret or transform existing archives; and the politics of preservation, access and knowledge production. Proposals should articulate a compelling approach to the archive plus a clear, connected pedagogical vision for how collective research unfolds over two years while supporting individual trajectories; partnerships with external organisations (museums, social archives, artist-led initiatives, community organisations and research institutions) are encouraged. FORMAT: Temporary Departments are full-time two-year master's studies bringing a cohort of students and tutors together around a specific field of inquiry; successful completion leads to a Master in Fine Arts and Design accredited by the NVAO. POSITION: the freelance Head of Department role is equivalent to approx. two days per week over the two academic years plus curriculum-development hours (October 2026 to July 2027), at an approximate daily rate of EUR 553.60, supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a department coordinator with administrative support, and institutional infrastructure. WHO CAN APPLY: applicants should have teaching/education experience; be able to develop and coordinate a two-year MA curriculum rooted in their own research and practice; have strong communication and leadership skills; have cross-disciplinary collaborative experience; be committed to an inclusive, supportive learning environment responsive to diverse access needs; be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate; hold an established independent professional practice registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KVK); and, if applying from outside the EU, hold a valid Dutch residence and work permit for the appointment. SELECTION (two rounds): first-round submissions to td@sandberg.nl by 17:00 on Sunday 5 July 2026 (a max. 500-word department proposal, a max. 200-word networks and collaborations statement, and a max. 2-page CV); first-round notification 15 July 2026; interviews with shortlisted candidates 16 September 2026. Questions: td@sandberg.nl.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Treehouse NDSM: Open Call, Come Back to Your ___ (Nature/Culture) Exhibition 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Treehouse NDSM

Location: Treehouse NDSM Pavilion, NDSM, Amsterdam-Noord, Netherlands; particular interest in artists from Amsterdam Noord.

Award: An artist fee is provided to all participating artists. Selected works are exhibited on the 100 m2 mezzanine of Treehouse NDSM's Pavilion. No application fee. (Artist fee amount not stated.)

Duration: Exhibition 17 September to 11 October 2026

Deadline: Closed 28 Jun 2026

Treehouse NDSM invites artists to apply for its next exhibition, 'Come Back to Your ___ (Nature/Culture)', running 17 September to 11 October 2026. In the context of current ecological crises, the exhibition takes the constant interaction between culture and nature as its starting point, noting how societies shape the land (from Japanese gardens to post-Soviet geometric parks, Dutch dikes and polders, and Indigenous Australian cultural burning) and how environments shape societies in turn. Artists are invited to reflect on questions such as: how do nature and culture share the same ground? How has your community shaped the land, and how has the land shaped your community? The exhibition is part of Art Park, a series launched at Treehouse in 2023 that reflects on urban nature, particularly in Amsterdam-Noord. The organisers welcome projects that do not offer answers but dig into questions and leave room for uncertainty, across media including performance, painting, making, installation and video. SELECTION: the committee (artist Alina Bielun and artistic coordinator Mulan Go) will select up to ten artworks for the 100 m2 mezzanine of Treehouse NDSM's Pavilion, considering both relevance to the theme and the chosen medium, with particular interest in the voices of artists from Amsterdam Noord (temporary or performative works that meaningfully complement the exhibition are also welcome). An artist fee is provided to all participants. Treehouse NDSM is not a traditional gallery but a multidisciplinary incubator offering 100 affordable studios, exhibition spaces and a collaborative artistic community. Submission deadline: 28 June 2026. Apply via the online form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kasteel Wijlre estate: The Resonant Garden Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Kasteel Wijlre estate

Location: Kasteel Wijlre estate, Wijlre, South Limburg, the Netherlands; open to artists living in the EU.

Award: Stipend of EUR 800 per month for three months (EUR 2,400 total) plus production and research compensation up to EUR 2,000. Private accommodation and a small in-house studio provided. All other costs (insurance, food, transport) covered by the resident. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, September to November 2026 (exact dates to be discussed)

Deadline: Closed 28 Jun 2026

Kasteel Wijlre estate invites international artists to apply for The Resonant Garden, a three-month residency set within the gardens and grounds of the estate in the hills of South Limburg. The garden is considered a place of cultivation and care, memory and imagination, rooted histories and possible futures; artists are invited to engage with it as a site for artistic research, hands-in-the-ground material experimentation and poetic speculation. Residents have access to the gardens and a small (16 sqm) in-house studio, with basic gardening and technical tools provided; the site itself, its green exterior, its historical views on 'the garden' and its embeddedness in the South Limburg landscape, becomes both companion and collaborator. Feedback sessions with garden and art professionals from the estate's network run throughout, concluding with an open studio event or presentation, and the residency sits within the Borderlands Residency network (networking and field trips). FOR WHOM: artists living in the EU with both research- and studio-based practices who wish to engage with the garden as a metaphor, method and/or material environment, and who embrace the rural location and the quiet and relative isolation of a country estate. ACCOMMODATION: private living and studio space (kitchen, bathroom, bedroom) in the castle courtyard; a bicycle is provided and the estate is reachable by public transport (Heerlen 12 km, Aachen 17 km, Maastricht 19 km). PRESENCE: the resident is expected to spend most of the period on site and to take part in at least two (semi-)public moments (an introductory presentation and a concluding open studio), and to be available for exchanges with the estate's professional network. BUDGET: EUR 800/month for three months plus production and research compensation up to EUR 2,000; all other costs (insurance, food, transport) are the resident's. SELECTION: a panel of two artist-advisors and the director-curator review applications on artistic vision, strength of proposal, and resonance with the estate context. APPLY: send a single PDF with a motivation statement and project proposal (max 500 words) and a CV (max 2 pages) to residency coordinator Anne Vangronsveld, a.vangronsveld@kasteelwijlre.nl. Deadline: 28 June 2026; decisions communicated the week of 6 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IMPAKT: Full Spectrum Curatorship Programme 2026
item.description:
Organisation: IMPAKT [Centre for Media Culture]

Location: Utrecht, Netherlands

Award: EUR 400 participation fee. Participants receive an EUR 800 stipend during the final phase to support preparation of their final project proposal.

Duration: September to December 2026; minimum 8 hours per week

Deadline: Closed 07 Jul 2026

The fourth edition of IMPAKT's Full Spectrum Curatorship Programme invites emerging and aspiring curators interested in media art, digital culture, and the intersection of technology and society to develop their own curatorial project proposal under expert guidance. Taking place in Utrecht from September to December 2026, it combines collective sessions, personal mentorship, guest contributions, and peer exchange, drawing on IMPAKT's archive and critical programming. Participants are paired with curator mentors to develop and position a curatorial proposal. The training covers the history, theory, contextualisation, organisation and execution of media-art programmes, including curatorial concept development, critical and societal contextualisation, artwork selection, budgets and funding, audience reception, and presentation formats, with a focus on time-based media and multi-channel/interactive formats. Confirmed mentors include Annet Dekker, Ine Gevers, Paulien Dresscher, Doreen A. Rios (online) and Eva Fischer (online). ELIGIBILITY: recent graduates and early- and mid-career curators; participants must attend all sessions and commit a minimum of 8 hours per week (preparation, research, independent work). All sessions are in English. COST AND SUPPORT: there is a EUR 400 participation fee, and participants receive an EUR 800 stipend in the final phase toward their project proposal. By the end, participants have a solid curatorial proposal to support future exhibitions and funding applications. Apply via the online form by 23:59 CET, 7 July 2026 (deadline extended from 1 July).

Open call details

item.title: Emergence Art+Science Fellowships: Open Call for New Commissions (Emergence Festival 2027)
item.description:
Organisation: Emergence at All Scales (EAAS), with The Science & Cocktails Foundation, Stichting Paradiso and NWA Route Kunst

Location: Netherlands (Amsterdam; premiere at the Emergence Festival)

Award: Production budget up to EUR 20,000 including VAT per project (covers artist fees, materials, equipment, space, travel, accommodation, installation and presentation). Total call budget EUR 80,000 (around four projects). No application fee.

Duration: Projects start September 2026; presentable output ready late May 2027; premiere June 2027

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Emergence at All Scales (EAAS), a Dutch research consortium spanning mathematics, physics, astronomy, chemistry and related fields (funded by NWO), invites artists, makers and collectives to propose new works inspired by the theme of Emergence: how complexity arises from simple ingredients, local interactions or microscopic rules. Selected projects are developed in dialogue with EAAS researchers and premiere at the first Emergence Festival in Amsterdam in June 2027. EAAS studies emergence across scales, from quantum spacetime and the early universe to hydrodynamics, quantum matter, collective behaviour, social segregation, networks and intelligent materials; proposals may connect to one project, several, or the theme broadly. WELCOMED FORMS: visual art, digital art, film, video, documentary, installation, exhibition, sculpture; performance, theatre, dance, music, sound art; and hybrid or interdisciplinary works that translate research questions, concepts, data, simulations, methods or early findings into a public experience, ideally with a life beyond the festival. FELLOWS RECEIVE: up to EUR 20,000 production budget, dialogue with EAAS researchers, support from EAAS curator Marijn Bril, possible university office/meeting space, festival presentation, and possible inclusion in the EAAS digital education and art database. REQUIREMENTS: a clear connection to Emergence and EAAS research that speaks to non-specialist audiences; evidence of contact with EAAS researchers during proposal development; a presentable output at the June 2027 festival; attendance at the EAAS annual meeting in March 2027 in Nijmegen; and a realistic technical/safety plan for festival presentation. TO APPLY by 30 June 2026: a proposal up to 3 pages (concept, research connection, proposed researchers/hosts, audience, work plan), a one-page budget, a CV, and a portfolio. Shortlist interviews July-August 2026. Questions: marijn@d-iep.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Jan van Eyck Academie: Material Research Fellowships 2027 (Future Materials)
item.description:
Organisation: Jan van Eyck Academie (Future Materials / Future Threads programme, co-funded by the EU Just Transition Fund)

Location: Maastricht, Netherlands (residency; fellows must reside within 10km)

Award: Monthly stipend of EUR 1,750 (80% exempt from income tax) plus an annual working budget of EUR 2,250, a private studio apartment, and access to the Jan van Eyck Labs and external partners. Application fee EUR 50 excl. VAT (waived for applicants from DAC-list lower-income countries and territories).

Duration: 11 months (15 January to 15 December 2027)

Deadline: Closed 24 Jun 2026

Two material-research fellowships at the Jan van Eyck Academie under the Future Materials programme: one Fashion & Textile Material Fellowship and one Future Materials Fellowship, each lasting 11 months. The programme supports environmentally conscious art and design practices researching sustainable, non-toxic, biobased alternatives to fossil-based or toxic materials. ELIGIBILITY: applicants with an existing research project on sustainable/biobased materials (the Fashion & Textile stream focuses on fashion/textile systems; the Future Materials stream on biobased materials or processes for broad creative use). Projects should take a systemic perspective on the just transition. Jan van Eyck alumni, and full-time alumni of the Rijksakademie or De Ateliers, are not eligible. Fellows develop their project with the Jan van Eyck Labs and Maastricht partners (Textile Innovation Maastricht; CHILL at Brightlands Chemelot Campus). Apply via the respective Jan van Eyck application form.

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Open Call Fresh Perspectives 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands (Dutch fund; cross-sector collaboration)

Award: Two-phase grant. Phase 1: ten grants of EUR 10,000 (max 5 months, startup 1 December 2026 to 30 April 2027) to prepare, explore and prototype the project. Phase 2: ten grants of EUR 50,000 (max 12 months, implementation 1 September 2027 to 31 August 2028) for projects selected and fully completed in Phase 1. No application fee.

Duration: Phase 1 max 5 months; Phase 2 (if selected) max 12 months

Deadline: 25 Aug 2026

Open call from the Creative Industries Fund NL (Stimuleringsfonds) for cross-sector collaboration projects that offer new perspectives on current challenges such as climate, housing, polarisation, migration or inequality of opportunity. The procedure runs in two phases. In Phase 1, the applicant and partner use the period to prepare and kick off the project, further explore the theme, intended end product and collaboration, and investigate possibilities (including working on a prototype or proof of concept), concluding with a project plan, budget and collaboration agreement. Phase 2, open only to projects selected and fully completed in Phase 1, focuses on implementation and requires both a written application and a presentation to the advisory committee; projects may deliver various end products (an activity, intervention, event, publication, service, product or process) accompanied by a suitable form of knowledge sharing such as a presentation, symposium or publication. ELIGIBILITY: applicants working with at least one partner in cross-sector collaboration; see the fund's open-call page for full eligibility details. The Phase 1 application window runs from 19 May 2026, 15:00 CEST to 25 August 2026, 16:00 CEST.

Open call details

item.title: NWO: SSH Open Competition XS 2026 - Round 3
item.description:
Organisation: Dutch Research Council (NWO)

Location: Netherlands (applicants must be academic researchers affiliated to a Dutch knowledge institution)

Award: Up to EUR 50,000 per project

Duration: Project-based, typically 12-24 months

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

Small curiosity-driven research grants in the social sciences and humanities, awarded by the Dutch Research Council (NWO). Round 3 of the 2026 SSH Open Competition XS closes 15 September 2026, 14:00 CEST. Up to EUR 50,000 per project to enable researchers to pursue novel, exploratory and high-risk ideas without the constraints of larger themed calls. ELIGIBILITY: academic researchers with an institutional affiliation at a Dutch university or recognised Dutch knowledge institute. Proposals are assessed on scientific quality and the potential for renewal of social-sciences and humanities research. Round 3 details and the dedicated application page will be published by NWO closer to the deadline; the linked page is the most recent SSH XS round, which is the same scheme.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Activity Programme Grant 2027-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands

Award: EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year for the 2027 and 2028 calendar years

Duration: Two years, 2027 and 2028

Deadline: Closed 15 Jun 2026

Two-year activity-programme funding from the Creative Industries Fund NL for organisations active in design, digital culture or architecture. Supports a coherent multi-year programme of activities running across 2027 and 2028 (rather than single projects). The funding underwrites a portion of the organisation's annual programme budget, giving recipients planning stability over the two-year period. Application window 13 May - 15 June 2026. EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year per organisation, depending on the scale and ambition of the proposed programme. ELIGIBILITY: cultural and creative-industries organisations established in the Netherlands working in design, digital culture or architecture; a distinct scheme from the Fund's annual project grants, festival rounds and voucher schemes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stroom Den Haag: PRO Grants 2026 (Research / Projects / Programmes / Investment)
item.description:
Organisation: Stroom Den Haag

Location: The Hague, Netherlands (eligibility restricted to professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague)

Award: Four schemes - PRO Research, PRO Projects, PRO Programmes, and PRO Investment - with amounts typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+ depending on scheme and proposal.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Stroom Den Haag's PRO grants support professional visual artists registered with Stroom in The Hague through four schemes: PRO Research (research and development for new work), PRO Projects (project realisation), PRO Programmes (multi-stage artistic programmes spanning research, production and presentation), and PRO Investment (investment in artistic practice, e.g. equipment, studio, professional development). Three remaining 2026 application rounds: 31 May, 6 September and 25 October 2026. ELIGIBILITY: professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague (tightly geographic). Amounts vary by scheme and proposal, typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Art Media Grant (October 2026 round)
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands (applicants must be part of the professional visual art field in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom)

Award: Up to 70% of eligible expenses. Round budget: €229,000. Two structures: fixed grant of €2,480 per month for 1-6 month work periods, OR a flexible grant of up to 12 months with the amount determined by project plan and budget.

Duration: 1 to 12 months, depending on project plan

Deadline: 15 Oct 2026

Mondriaan Fund grant for written and/or spoken-word publications about contemporary visual art: article series, long reads, podcasts, video essays, social-content series, and similar formats published across magazines, newspapers, online platforms, public media or social media. Aimed at strengthening reflection on, criticism of, and journalism around visual art practice in the Netherlands and the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. Eligible applicants: existing or new platforms (online magazines, public media, podcast outlets, etc.) AND individuals working as curators, critics, social-content creators or journalists. New initiatives and collaborations may apply. Two cycles per year; the next deadline is 15 October 2026, 16:00 Dutch time / 10:00 Caribbean time (an earlier 2 April 2026 round has already closed). Grant covers up to 70% of eligible expenses, with two pricing structures: fixed €2,480/month for short 1-6 month work periods, or a flexible grant of up to 12 months for larger project plans (amount determined by submitted plan and budget). Round budget €229,000. Good fit for science-communicator / critical-tech / art-and-AI long-form writing, podcast series, or video essay projects. Applications submitted via the Mondriaan Fund online portal after creating an account; documents cannot be uploaded after the deadline. Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pictoright Fonds: 2026 Project Funding Rounds
item.description:
Organisation: Pictoright Fonds

Location: Netherlands (NL-based visual makers)

Award: Up to €35,000 per project. Applications above €5,000 must show substantial co-financing from other sources.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: Closed 07 Jun 2026

Pictoright's collective rights fund supports projects with a social, cultural or educational purpose serving a broad group of professional visual makers in design/illustration, photography, and fine art working/residing in the Netherlands. Six to seven rounds per year; remaining 2026 deadlines: 7 June, 23 August, 4 October, 8 November. Decisions communicated within ~6 weeks of each deadline. Maximum grant €35,000; for requests above €5,000 the project must be substantially co-financed by other sources. The remit is collective benefit (publications, infrastructure, sector-wide research, exhibitions, education, professional development for the field), not individual practice grants, so projects need to articulate how they serve other visual makers, not just the applicant. Worth tracking for any artist-organised research, publication, exhibition, training or open-source-toolkit project benefitting the Dutch visual-maker community.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Festivals Creative Industries 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie

Location: Netherlands

Award: Per-festival grant; specific maximum per Festivals scheme rules (cash subsidy, not in-kind)

Duration: Festival edition in 2027

Deadline: 23 Sep 2026

Annual scheme funding festival organisations in the creative industries (design, architecture, digital culture, fashion, e-culture, etc.) presenting a 2027 edition. Application window opens 25 August 2026 at 15:00 CEST and closes 23 September 2026 at 16:00 CEST. Supports both content programming and the organisational/curatorial running of a festival; applicants must be organisations rather than individuals, but the scheme is the natural home for any artist-led collective or platform that runs a critical-AI / digital-culture / surveillance-focused festival event. Note the wider 2026 context: Stimuleringsfonds is restructuring its grant offering for 2027, and several individual schemes (Design, Digital Culture) have been reduced to two rounds in 2026. The Festivals scheme is a separate annual track and one of the cleaner ways to access SCI funding for an event-format project.

Open call details

Multi-year grant for cultural institutions whose core task is to contribute to the high quality, development and professionalisation of the contemporary creative industry through a two-year activities programme. item.title: International Vouchers
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands (for international travel)

Award: Up to €1,500 within Europe (zone 1) / up to €2,500 outside Europe (zone 2)

Duration: Per trip

Deadline: 27 Oct 2026

Travel voucher for Dutch-based professionals in design, architecture and/or digital culture who have been invited by a foreign party to give a presentation, lecture or workshop. To apply you must have submitted an application to the Fund in the past five years that was positively assessed (previous International Vouchers do not count). The inviting foreign party must cover a proportionate share of the costs. Covers hourly fees, travel and accommodation directly related to the activity. IMPORTANT TIMING RULE: applications are accepted from 5 months to a minimum of 7 weeks before the activity begins, and processing takes about 6 weeks. This lead time can rule out activities that fall shortly after a round opens. Round 2 (4 May to 22 June 2026) has closed. Round 3 runs 8 September 2026 15:00 CEST to 27 October 2026 16:00 CEST, with a budget of EUR 33,000; given the 7-week rule it effectively serves activities starting from late October 2026 onwards.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

Talent prize for recently graduated bachelor filmmakers from a Dutch film or art academy (documentary, fiction or animation). item.title: Stichting Grensloos: Dichter/bij Weiteveen Open Call
item.description:

Location: Weiteveen, Southeast Drenthe, Netherlands

Award: €3,500 artist fee + €1,000 materials + €0.23/km travel (all incl. VAT)

Duration: Exhibition runs 20 August to 3 October 2026

Deadline: Closed 01 May 2026

Stichting Grensloos invites an artist or artists to create a work for the exhibition Dichter/bij in Weiteveen, Southeast Drenthe. Open to professional artists (self-taught also welcome) working solo, duo, or as a collective, across all disciplines. Crossover practitioners and community-art makers are explicitly encouraged. VAT), and €0.23/km travel reimbursement (limited public transport in the area).

item.title: Call for Proposals: Alex Adriaansens Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: V2_ Lab for the Unstable Media

Location: Rotterdam, Netherlands

Award: €7,500

Duration: 3 months (starts 1 September 2026)

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Residency honouring V2_ co-founder Alex Adriaansens, aimed at upcoming curators, festival/programme initiators, community organisers and cultural practitioners in media art, digital cultures and related fields who are starting their professional careers. Duos or teams may apply (sharing the grant). No thematic requirement; applicants choose their own research focus and have access to V2_'s extensive archive. Apply with a letter of motivation (max 2 pages), short CV (max 1 page) and a specific research question (max 1 page) via the linked Google form, or email v2@v2.nl if you do not have a Google account. A jury of V2_ team members decides. Results will be presented in Rotterdam at a jointly organised event (e.g. expert meeting), or via an essay, or another format proposed by the resident.

Open call details

item.description:

Location: Rotterdam, Netherlands (Rotterdam-based artists only)

Award: Up to €7,500 (time compensation; fee depends on project duration)

Duration: Defined work period or project

Deadline: 21 Sep 2026

Subsidy supporting in-depth activities or innovative impulses within the artistic practice of Rotterdam-based visual artists, with a clearly defined artistic question or objective. Applicants must be registered with Art Office (artoffice.info) with an up-to-date artist page. The 2026 Round 2 deadline is 21 September 2026, 11:00 (form opens 10 August 2026); results by 16 November 2026. Round 1 (deadline 2 March 2026) has already closed. Decisions made by the director of CBK Rotterdam on advice from an expert committee.

item.title: Making Visible the Invisible: Critical Pathways Fellowships 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Critical Pathways community and Towards a Circular Economy and Society, Utrecht University

Location: Utrecht, Netherlands

Award: Up to €10,000 (travel, accommodation, living expenses, visa)

Duration: 4 to 6 weeks (Oct to Nov 2026, dates flexible)

Deadline: Closed 27 Apr 2026

Fellowship for anyone whose work uncovers hidden stories, systems and power structures behind ecological breakdown and inequality, and reimagines fairer alternatives. Open to artists, filmmakers, writers, activists, organisers, journalists, legal practitioners, policy advocates, conservationists, sustainability practitioners, entrepreneurs and academics from any discipline. No PhD required. 2026 theme: Making Visible the Invisible (overlooked consequences of how we live, produce and consume; who bears hidden costs; shifting from extractive to regenerative systems). Apply by sending the form (.docx) plus CV to criticalpathways@uu.nl. Notifications within 6 weeks of deadline.

Open call details
